diagnostics.consequence: Tournament rankings may overstate hypothesis quality if the auto-evaluator shares the same blind spots as the generation process, because the reported Elo score is not anchored to independent ground truth.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Note that the Elo metric is auto-evaluated and not based on independent ground truth.

diagnostics.statement: The Elo-based evaluation mechanism is explicitly auto-evaluated rather than independently grounded.

diagnostics.consequence: A hypothesis can be discarded before external prior-art or evidence checks are applied, creating a risk of false rejection for hypotheses that only appear flawed or non-novel under internal review.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: This initial review, which doesn’t use external tools like web search, aims to quickly discard flawed, non-novel, or otherwise unsuitable hypotheses.

The Reflection agent searches relevant prior work (via web search or a dedicated scientist-provided repository), assesses existing experimental evidence for or against a given hypothesis, and rigorously verifies the novelty, correctness, and quality of generated outputs.

diagnostics.statement: The Reflection workflow includes an initial no-external-tool review that can discard hypotheses, even though later Reflection capabilities include prior-work search and evidence assessment.

diagnostics.consequence: The packet supports external validation and persistent memory, but it does not establish a closed-loop architecture in which wet-lab observations modify later autonomous hypothesis generation or define autonomous escalation to experiments.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The co-scientist’s hypotheses for these three settings are externally, independently validated by in vitro laboratory experiments and detailed in separate preprints co-timed with this work.

Context memory. In order to enable iterative computation and scientific reasoning over long time horizons, the co-scientist uses a persistent context memory to store and retrieve states of the agents and the system during the course of the computation.

This work does not aim to completely automate the scientific process with AI. Instead, the co-scientist is purpose-built for a “scientist-in-the-loop” collaborative paradigm, to help domain experts augment their hypothesis generation process and guide the exploration that follows.

diagnostics.statement: The packet does not report how external wet-lab validation results feed back into context memory or later autonomous decisions, and it frames the system as scientist-in-the-loop rather than fully automated.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: Note that the Elo metric is auto-evaluated and not based on independent ground truth.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: At least one independent ground-truth or external validation benchmark is reported, with its relationship to Elo explicitly quantified.
success_check.evidence_needed: Benchmark protocol, independent labels or outcomes, and reported comparison between Elo ranking and those independent outcomes.
success_check.observable: Independent evidence supporting or contradicting Elo-ranked hypothesis quality.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: This initial review, which doesn’t use external tools like web search, aims to quickly discard flawed, non-novel, or otherwise unsuitable hypotheses.

The Reflection agent searches relevant prior work (via web search or a dedicated scientist-provided repository), assesses existing experimental evidence for or against a given hypothesis, and rigorously verifies the novelty, correctness, and quality of generated outputs.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: 100% of hypotheses rejected for non-novelty or evidentiary unsuitability have a logged prior-art/evidence check before final rejection.
success_check.evidence_needed: Reflection-stage decision logs showing rejection reasons and whether web search or scientist-provided repository checks occurred before final discard.
success_check.observable: Prior-art or external-evidence verification before irreversible hypothesis discard.

exact_quotes.exact_quote: The co-scientist’s hypotheses for these three settings are externally, independently validated by in vitro laboratory experiments and detailed in separate preprints co-timed with this work.

Context memory. In order to enable iterative computation and scientific reasoning over long time horizons, the co-scientist uses a persistent context memory to store and retrieve states of the agents and the system during the course of the computation.

This work does not aim to completely automate the scientific process with AI. Instead, the co-scientist is purpose-built for a “scientist-in-the-loop” collaborative paradigm, to help domain experts augment their hypothesis generation process and guide the exploration that follows.

success_check.comparator_or_threshold: Documentation explicitly answers all three boundary questions: wet-lab feedback-to-memory, autonomous escalation rule, and instrument/robot control; otherwise mark each as not reported.
success_check.evidence_needed: Architecture or methods text describing post-validation data flow, decision authority for wet-lab escalation, and whether laboratory instruments are controlled by the co-scientist.
success_check.observable: Clear boundary statement for the relationship between computational agents, human scientists, context memory, and wet-lab execution.

memo: Implemented mechanisms reported in the packet include persistent context memory for storing and retrieving agent/system state, a Reflection agent that can search prior work and assess novelty/correctness/quality, an initial Reflection review that discards flawed or unsuitable hypotheses without external tools, tournament ranking feeding top-ranked hypotheses to an Evolution agent, auto-evaluated Elo scoring, and external independent in vitro validation for three reported settings. Key architectural concerns are that Elo is not independently grounded, early discard can precede external prior-art checks, and the packet does not report a closed wet-lab feedback or autonomous lab-control loop.
